title: NOC 72311 – Cabinetmakers | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Cabinetmakers (NOC 72311) are the precision artisans of Canada’s interior construction and furniture sectors. As the demand for high-end custom millwork and "Smart Kitchens" surges, these professionals are transitioning from traditional woodworking to a hybrid of manual craft and Digital Fabrication. Operating at the TEER 2 level, they specialize in the construction, repair, and restyling of wooden cabinets, furniture, and fixtures. In 2026, the industry is increasingly focused on sustainable materials and the use of CNC (Computer Numerical Control) machinery to achieve the complex geometries required in modern Canadian architecture.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Category2 (Usually requires a college diploma, apprenticeship, or significant experience)Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officers and controllersSub-Major Group723 – Technical construction trades

duties: Technical Analysis: Studying plans, specifications, or blueprints to determine the layout and material requirements for custom articles.
Precision Machining: Operating woodworking machines such as power saws, jointers, mortisers, and shapers to cut and form parts.
Complex Assembly: Trimming joints and fitting parts together using glue, clamps, nails, and screws to form complete units.
Surface Finishing: Sanding wooden surfaces and applying veneers, stains, or polishes to prepare the final product for installation.
Restoration: Repairing or restyling high-value wooden furniture and fixtures for commercial and residential clients.
2026 Update: Utilizing CADD software to design and estimate material costs and programming CNC machines for high-precision batch production.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Apprenticeship: Completion of a four-year apprenticeship program OR a combination of over four years of work experience and some college courses is required for trade certification.
Certification: Trade certification is available but voluntary in all provinces. However, in B.C., certification is highly encouraged through SkilledTradesBC.
Red Seal: The Interprovincial Standards Red Seal is the "Gold Standard," allowing for mobility across all Canadian provinces and higher wage brackets.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is Moderate to Good, driven by a stable renovation market and the luxury custom-home sector:

Ontario: Moderate prospects. In February 2026, Ontario issued targeted OINP invitations for NOC 72311 under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker stream. Candidates with a job offer in Central Ontario (excluding the GTA) and a score of 55+ were prioritized, highlighting a need for skilled woodworkers in regional hubs.

British Columbia: Strong demand. Cabinetmakers are currently on the BC PNP Priority Occupations List for construction. B.C. projects steady growth in the specialized millwork sector, with annual earnings averaging around $50,500.

Alberta: Steady demand. While average wages are slightly lower than the national peak (median around $23.00/hr in cities like Edmonton), the low cost of living and robust residential construction make it a popular choice for newcomers.
National Trend: Over 92% of cabinetmakers work full-time. Roughly 28% of professionals in this NOC are self-employed, which is nearly double the average for all other occupations.
Why Work in Canada as a Cabinetmaker?
Lead the Digital Woodshop: Gain expertise in 2026-grade technologies, including CNC routers, laser-edge banders, and 3D modeling software for custom kitchen designs.
High-End Niche Growth: Specialize in the booming "Aging-in-Place" market, building custom accessible cabinetry for Canada's senior population.
Entrepreneurial Freedom: With one of the highest self-employment rates in the trades, Canada offers a clear pathway to opening your own custom furniture or cabinetry studio.
Priority for PR: In early 2026, this NOC has been a consistent highlight in BC PNP and OINP draws, offering a faster route to Permanent Residency for certified workers.
pathways: OINP Employer Job Offer: In February 2026, Ontario issued invitations to candidates in NOC 72311 with a score of 55+ (Central Ontario). This is an ideal route for those with an Ontario job offer.
BC PNP Construction Stream: As a Priority Occupation, cabinetmakers benefit from targeted invitations and faster processing if they have a valid B.C. job offer and apprenticeship registration.

Express Entry (Trade Occupations Category): Cabinetmakers are eligible for category-based draws for skilled trades. Note that for 2026, IRCC now requires 12 months of full-time experience (up from 6 months).
2026 TR to PR Pathway: A confirmed federal initiative targeting up to 33,000 temporary residents. Cabinetmakers in the construction sector are classified as "High-Priority Infrastructure Workers."
